question: Write a C++ program that declares a 3x3 integer matrix. Prompt the user to enter values for each element of the matrix. After filling the matrix, calculate and print the sum of all elements in the matrix.
errors: The loops for calculating the sum use incorrect bounds (i <= 3 and j <= 3). This attempts to access elements matrix[3][...] and matrix[...][3], which are outside the declared array bounds of a 3x3 matrix (valid indices are 0, 1, 2). This results in undefined behavior, potentially leading to a crash or the summation of garbage values from adjacent memory locations.
